verdict_reasoning: BRAF is a cytoplasmic serine/threonine kinase of the RAF family. It acts as a soluble intracellular effector in the MAPK/ERK signaling cascade. While BRAF transiently associates with the plasma membrane via interaction with membrane-anchored RAS during signal transduction, it lacks its own membrane anchor—no transmembrane domain, GPI anchor, or stable lipidation. Per the cardinal recruitment test, transient non-covalent recruitment to another surface protein does not qualify as BRAF's own mechanism for reaching the outer leaflet.
